response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer (web, mobile, embedded tooling) with strong testing discipline and some low-latency/high-throughput backend experience, but the role demands hardware-centric expertise the resume does not show: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, and hardware-software co-design. There is no evidence of deep-learning workload optimization, silicon architecture, or chip-level performance work, so the profile does not meet the core requirements for a Principal Performance Architect.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering career with low-latency/high-throughput backend experience (Dunnhumby microservices)
Some systems-level C/C++ exposure via National Instruments LabWindows/CVI and embedded work
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference workload or hardware-software co-design background
Profile is application/web software, not principal-level hardware performance architecture
